taxonomy.description: The model consistently assigns a lower classification (e.g., Low, Medium) than the actual complaint's severity or urgency (e.g., Medium, High). This often occurs due to failing to synthesize cumulative negative factors, overlooking critical escalation triggers, customer vulnerability, significant impact, or explicit demands.
original_error_titles: Underestimation of Cumulative Urgency and Severity Indicators
Failure to Recognize Escalation and Formal Threats
Misinterpretation of Complaint Severity and Escalation Signals
Underestimation of Sentiment Severity and Customer Impact
Underestimation of Urgency and Customer Vulnerability
Failure to Recognize Explicit Escalation Triggers and Threats
Underestimation of Critical Impact and Cumulative Factors
Failure to Synthesize Contextual Details for Severity Assessment
Underestimation of Cumulative Negative Impact and Distress
Failure to recognize escalation risk and combined severity indicators
Failure to identify critical urgency indicators
Underestimation of Urgency Despite Clear Churn Threat and Failed Resolutions
Failure to Identify Key Urgency Indicators
Underestimation of Business Impact and Recurrence
Underestimation of Cumulative Severity and Urgency Signals
Failure to Synthesize Multiple Priority Factors
Failure to identify and weigh escalation indicators
Underestimation of Cumulative Severity and Customer Impact
Underestimation of Escalation Triggers and Customer Urgency
Underestimation of Severity and Urgency Indicators
Underestimation of Textual Complexity and Formality
Underestimation of Issue Severity and Resolution Complexity
Underestimation of Cumulative Error Severity and Customer Impact
Underestimating Impact of Recurring Issues and Unfulfilled Promises
Underestimation of Sentiment Severity and Escalation Indicators
Underestimation of Issue Impact and Urgency

taxonomy.description: The model consistently assigns a higher classification (e.g., Medium, High) than the actual complaint's severity or urgency (e.g., Low, Medium). This is typically due to over-emphasizing subjective language, non-critical factors, or misapplying internal thresholds for what constitutes a 'High' or 'Medium' issue, often by overlooking mitigating circumstances or objective criteria.
original_error_titles: Misinterpretation of Escalation and Urgency Cues
Severity Threshold Misjudgment (Ombudsman vs. High Escalation)
Misinterpretation of Urgency Indicators and Impact Severity
Misinterpretation of Customer Vulnerability as High Urgency
Inaccurate Severity Calibration for Health/Safety Risks
Overestimation of Urgency from Subjective Language and Insufficient Objective Context Evaluation
Overemphasis on Formal Language and Standard Escalation
Misjudged Urgency of Future Service Cessation
Overemphasis on Contact Frequency and Customer Frustration over Actual Impact and Threat Level
Misinterpretation of Urgency Based on Timeframe and Immediacy of Risk
Misinterpreting Priority Tiers based on Scope of Impact
Misinterpretation of Urgency Threshold (High Risk vs. Immediate Emergency)
Overemphasis on Subjective Experience vs. Objective Impact
Overestimation of Urgency Without Escalatory Cues
Misinterpretation of Escalation and Impact for Urgency Level
Overemphasis on distress over actual impact for urgency classification
Overemphasis on Subjective Impact/Frustration over Objective System Status
Underweighting of Resolution Timeframe and Severity of Disruption
Overweighting External Escalation Threat and Underweighting Mitigating Factors
Misinterpretation of Politeness in Assertive Complaint
Overemphasis on inconvenience over core issue criticality
Underestimation of Negative Emotional Intensity and Complaint Severity
Underestimation of Sentiment Severity from Cumulative Negative Events and Escalation Signals

taxonomy.category_name: Failure to Accurately Process Specific Information Cues or Adhere to Instructions
taxonomy.description: The model struggles with correctly identifying, interpreting, or utilizing specific types of information present in the text, such as explicit instructions, detailed reference data, formal language indicators, or the precise nature of external escalation bodies. This category also includes instances where the model fails to adhere to explicit content generation or detail level requirements.
original_error_titles: Provided General Information Instead of Strict Redirection
Failure to Adhere to Explicit Instruction
Failure to Identify External Escalation Reference
Misinterpretation of external escalation body and its severity
Failure to Recognize Explicit Escalation Triggers
Failure to weigh mitigating factors for complaint severity
